text: Welcome to The Long View. Today we're exploring sustainability in depth with Dr. Elena Fischer, sustainability researcher and systems thinker.
Thanks, Alex Morgan. This is a topic I've spent decades on.

Let's talk about systems thinking. What's your read?

Honestly, Sustainability is a systems-thinking problem before it's an environmental problem. Teams working with Carbon Trust run into this constantly. Most of the cost shows up downstream, which is why teams keep paying it.

That ties to another point — Most sustainability framing fails because it treats outcomes as targets instead of properties of systems. I've watched this pattern hold across three different teams and the underlying dynamic doesn't change.
